description_long: This is a pair of metal pliers with a robust, utilitarian design, commonly used for gripping, bending, or cutting wires and other materials. The handles are long and slightly curved, providing leverage and a comfortable grip, though they lack any visible insulation or ergonomic padding. The jaws are broad and flat with a slight taper, featuring a textured gripping surface for enhanced hold on objects. The pivot point is a simple fixed joint, allowing the two arms to open and close smoothly. The entire tool has a dark, matte metallic finish, suggesting it is made primarily of steel or iron. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, emphasizing its functional, industrial character.
description: A sturdy, dark metal pair of pliers with curved handles and broad, textured jaws, designed for gripping and manipulating materials. The tool has a matte finish and a utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the pliers, highlighting the curved handles and the broad, flat jaws.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the open jaws and the symmetrical curvature of the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the handles and jaws from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the jaws and the open handles, emphasizing the pivot joint.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, uninsulated metal handles with a smooth contour for basic grip.
attribute_jaw_design: Broad, flat jaws with a slight taper and textured gripping surfaces; no wire cutter or special notches.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed single pivot joint, non-adjustable, allowing standard opening and closing motion.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal with no visible ribbing, knurling, or rubberized texture.
